perturbation.original_text: 4. Obligation of Confidentiality. For a period of 3 years from the date of disclosure, Recipient agrees to use the same care and discretion as it uses with its own c onfidential information to avoid, except as expressly permitted in this Agreement, disclosure or publication of Discloser’s Confidential Information. At a minimum shall Recipient use reasonable care to protect Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Obligation of Confidentiality. Recipient agrees to use discretion to avoid disclosure or publication of Discloser’s Confidential Information. At a minimum shall Recipient use reasonable care to protect Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause, 'For a period of 3 years from the date of disclosure, Recipient agrees to use the same care and discretion as it uses with its own c onfidential information to avoid, except as expressly permitted in this Agreement,' the duration of the confidentiality obligation is no longer defined. This creates a contradiction because the agreement now lacks clarity on how long the confidentiality lasts. This means that section 7 (Termination) is not clear when the obligations of confidentiality shall end.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Termination. This Agreement shall begin on the date it is executed and shall extend until that date which is six (6) months after the date on which Individual ceases to be a participant in TTP. Either party may terminate this Agreement for its convenience upon wri tten notice to the other. Upon termination of this Agreement, or earlier upon Discloser’s request, Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all documents and tangible items in its possession which contain any part of the Confidential Information of Discloser. Upon Agreement termination, all obligations of confidentia lity shall survive and cont inue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 3-year confidentiality period, while the changed text removes this duration. Section 7 (Termination) states that confidentiality obligations survive termination 'in accordance with their terms.' Without a defined duration in section 4 due to the omission, it becomes unclear how long the confidentiality obligations last after termination of the agreement, creating a direct contradiction and making enforcement uncertain. The change directly contradicts the enforceability of the termination section because the terms of the confidentiality are no longer clear. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed creating a contradiction with obligations in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Freedom of Use. Recipient shall be free to use for any purposes Residuals resulting from access to or work with Discloser’s Confidential Information. Howeve r, the foregoing does not give Recipient the right to disclose the financial, statistical, or personnel informat ion or the business plans of Discloser, and shall not be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other par ty’s copyrights or patents. The term "Residuals" means information which may be retained in non-tangi ble form by persons who have had access to the Confidential Information, including without limitati on general ideas, concepts, know-how or techniques contained therein. Neither party shall have any obligat ion to limit or restrict the assignment or reassignment of personnel. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, t he parties acknowledge that the communications hereunder will not serve to impair the right of either party to independently develop, make, use, procure or market products or services now or in the future that may be similar to or competitive with those offered by Discloser, nor require Recipient to disclose any planning or other information to Discloser. This Section shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Freedom of Use. Recipient shall be free to use for any purposes Residuals resulting from access to or work with Discloser’s Confidential Information. Howeve r, the foregoing does not give Recipient the right to disclose the financial, statistical, or personnel informat ion or the business plans of Discloser, and shall not be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other par ty’s copyrights or patents. Neither party shall have any obligat ion to limit or restrict the assignment or reassignment of personnel. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, t he parties acknowledge that the communications hereunder will not serve to impair the right of either party to independently develop, make, use, procure or market products or services now or in the future that may be similar to or competitive with those offered by Discloser, nor require Recipient to disclose any planning or other information to Discloser. This Section shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'Residuals', the term becomes ambiguous. While Recipient is still allowed to use residuals, the lack of definition means that the specific permitted uses of that information now lack clarity. This contradicts the rest of the section because the restrictions placed on 'financial, statistical, or personnel information' depends on knowing the difference between those data types and 'residuals'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Freedom of Use. Recipient shall be free to use for any purposes Residuals resulting from access to or work with Discloser’s Confidential Information. Howeve r, the foregoing does not give Recipient the right to disclose the financial, statistical, or personnel informat ion or the business plans of Discloser, and shall not be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other par ty’s copyrights or patents. Neither party shall have any obligat ion to limit or restrict the assignment or reassignment of personnel. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, t he parties acknowledge that the communications hereunder will not serve to impair the right of either party to independently develop, make, use, procure or market products or services now or in the future that may be similar to or competitive with those offered by Discloser, nor require Recipient to disclose any planning or other information to Discloser. This Section shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Residuals,' clarifying the scope of permissible use. The changed text removes this definition, creating ambiguity about what constitutes 'Residuals.' This contradicts the section also labeled as 'Contradicted Text' (which is the same section as the changed text) because the restrictions placed on 'financial, statistical, or personnel information' rely on a clear distinction from 'Residuals'. Without the definition, the permitted uses of residuals become unclear, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Termination. This Agreement shall begin on the date it is executed and shall extend until that date which is six (6) months after the date on which Individual ceases to be a participant in TTP. Either party may terminate this Agreement for its convenience upon wri tten notice to the other. Upon termination of this Agreement, or earlier upon Discloser’s request, Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all documents and tangible items in its possession which contain any part of the Confidential Information of Discloser. Upon Agreement termination, all obligations of confidentia lity shall survive and cont inue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Termination. This Agreement shall begin on the date it is executed. Either party may terminate this Agreement for its convenience upon wri tten notice to the other. Upon termination of this Agreement, or earlier upon Discloser’s request, Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all documents and tangible items in its possession which contain any part of the Confidential Information of Discloser. Upon Agreement termination, all obligations of confidentia lity shall survive and cont inue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and shall extend until that date which is six (6) months after the date on which Individual ceases to be a participant in TTP.' the end date of the agreement is unclear. The statement 'Upon Agreement termination, all obligations of confidentiality shall survive and continue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.' now contradicts the rest of the section because the exact obligations of confidentiality are defined to end upon 'Agreement termination', but the 'Agreement termination' date isn't provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Termination. This Agreement shall begin on the date it is executed. Either party may terminate this Agreement for its convenience upon wri tten notice to the other. Upon termination of this Agreement, or earlier upon Discloser’s request, Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all documents and tangible items in its possession which contain any part of the Confidential Information of Discloser. Upon Agreement termination, all obligations of confidentia lity shall survive and cont inue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the agreement would last until six months after the Individual ceased participation in TTP. The changed text removes this end date, making the agreement's duration unclear. The contradicted text states that confidentiality obligations survive 'Agreement termination', but because the termination date is no longer definitively stated, the extent of these surviving obligations becomes ambiguous, creating a direct conflict and strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. General. a. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in ac cordance with the laws of Utah (without regard to conflicts of laws provisions). In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonabl e attorneys' fees. The parties agree that Utah state and federal courts shall have jurisd iction and venue in any such proceeding. b. Export Constraints. Recipient shall follow U.S. Ex port Administration laws and regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products rece ived from Discloser or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed country listed in the U.S. Export Administration regulations unless properly authorized by the U.S. government. c. Neither party may assign its rights or delegate its du ties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. The parties acknowledge that they have read this Agreement, understand it, and agree to be b ound by the terms and conditions. Further, they agree that the complete and exclus ive statement of the agreement between the parties relating to this subject shall consist of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. General. a. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in ac cordance with the laws of Utah (without regard to conflicts of laws provisions). In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonabl e attorneys' fees. b. Export Constraints. Recipient shall follow U.S. Ex port Administration laws and regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products rece ived from Discloser or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed country listed in the U.S. Export Administration regulations unless properly authorized by the U.S. government. c. Neither party may assign its rights or delegate its du ties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. The parties acknowledge that they have read this Agreement, understand it, and agree to be b ound by the terms and conditions. Further, they agree that the complete and exclus ive statement of the agreement between the parties relating to this subject shall consist of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'The parties agree that Utah state and federal courts shall have jurisd iction and venue in any such proceeding.' from section 10, the venue for disputes becomes unclear. While the agreement is governed by Utah law, the lack of a specific venue clause now creates an inconsistency. Specifically the sentence, 'This Agreement shall be governed and construed in ac cordance with the laws of Utah (without regard to conflicts of laws provisions).', implies Utah Law, but, since the venue has been removed, contradicts the overall enforcement and interpretation of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. General. a. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in ac cordance with the laws of Utah (without regard to conflicts of laws provisions). In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonabl e attorneys' fees. b. Export Constraints. Recipient shall follow U.S. Ex port Administration laws and regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products rece ived from Discloser or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed country listed in the U.S. Export Administration regulations unless properly authorized by the U.S. government. c. Neither party may assign its rights or delegate its du ties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. The parties acknowledge that they have read this Agreement, understand it, and agree to be b ound by the terms and conditions. Further, they agree that the complete and exclus ive statement of the agreement between the parties relating to this subject shall consist of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that Utah state and federal courts have jurisdiction and venue. The changed text omits this sentence, while another section in the contract still states that the agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Utah. This creates a contradiction because, while Utah law governs the agreement, there is no specified venue for disputes, making the enforcement of Utah law uncertain and potentially conflicting with other possible venues. This aligns with the provided definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
